source_test: HEC.FDA.Model/metrics/AggregatedConsequencesBinned.cs's ConvertToSingleEmpiricalDistributionOfConsequences + StudyAreaConsequencesBinned.cs's ConvertToStudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile (no upstream unit test file exists for either; oracle-gate-reproduced against the real methods directly)
note: Phase 6 Task 4 (un-severed at Phase 4 Tasks 3/4). `consequence_results` is a list of {construct: {damage_category, asset_category, convergence: {min_iterations, max_iterations}, impact_area_id, consequence_type, risk_type}, realizations: [{iteration, damage, count}, ...]}, exactly the aggregated_consequences_binned.json construct/realizations shape -- one AggregatedConsequencesBinned per entry, staged via add_consequence_realization then put_data_into_histogram (Phase 4 Task 3 surface), then collected into a StudyAreaConsequencesBinned via its (vector<AggregatedConsequencesBinned>) ctor. `filter_consequence_type` is the ConsequenceType passed to convert_to_study_area_consequences_by_quantile (a plain `==` filter over each result's own ConsequenceType, NOT the wildcard-aware filter_by_categories predicate -- see study_area_consequences_binned.hpp). The resulting StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile's sample_mean_damage(damage_category_or_null, asset_category_or_null, impact_area_id) is asserted (args order matches study_area_consequences_by_quantile.json's convention); since each AggregatedConsequencesByQuantile's Empirical.SampleMean is force-set (inside convert_to_empirical_distribution, Task 1) to the SOURCE histogram's own SampleMean rather than recomputed from the 2500-point empirical sweep, sample_mean_damage after conversion tracks the pre-conversion binned sample mean exactly, transitively through the ByQuantile collection's own summation. Cases: (1) single_damage_result -- one Damage-type binned result, IterationCount pinned to 1 (MaxIterations < 100 quirk, same as aggregated_consequences_binned.json's single_iteration_narrow_range), verifying the single-object conversion path (AggregatedConsequencesBinned::convert_to_single_empirical_distribution_of_consequences) end to end through both an exact-match and a fully-wildcarded sample_mean_damage query; (2) mixed_types_filter_excludes_lifeloss -- three binned results sharing damage_category/impact_area_id: two Damage-type (Structure, Content -- reusing aggregated_consequences_binned.json's multi_iteration_wide_range and single_iteration_narrow_range realization sets respectively, under new asset categories) and one LifeLoss-type sharing the SAME (damage_category, asset_category) tuple as the Structure/Damage result (reusing aggregated_consequences_binned.json's negative_and_mixed_values realizations) -- filtering by ConsequenceType::Damage must exclude the LifeLoss result entirely from the converted StudyAreaConsequencesByQuantile (not merely deprioritize it), verified by a fully-wildcarded sample_mean_damage summing to ONLY the two Damage sample means, plus two category-scoped queries isolating each Damage result. All `expected` values PIN (gate-captured, never hand-derived) -- DynamicHistogram::convert_to_empirical_distribution's SampleMean pass-through makes the expected numbers structurally predictable (should match the sample means an aggregated_consequences_binned fixture with the same realizations would report), but are still gate-pinned per project convention rather than cross-referenced by hand.
